repo_prior_art.cite: doc/API.md §3.1 t3_thm is bound algebra/intersection containment, not endpoint inference

published_prior_art.cite: ChernozhukovLeeRosen2013: intersection bounds as sup/inf over infinite constraints with active-set Gaussian inference; FangSantos2019: directionally differentiable delta method

repo_prior_art.cite: doc/API.md §3.1 t3_thm has no contact-set phase law

published_prior_art.cite: ChernozhukovLeeRosen2013: inference driven by active argmax set V0, including singleton/finite sharp cases; continuum case is the same sup over V0
FangSantos2019: standard bootstrap consistency iff full differentiability for Gaussian limits; max/tie failure is a direct special case

structure_flags.label: Introduction
structure_flags.one_line: The required four-paragraph introduction is not followed exactly: question and main claim are split into separate paragraphs before the comparator and consumer paragraphs.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The contact-cone derivative is the standard supremum-functional active-set derivative, already covered for intersection bounds by ChernozhukovLeeRosen2013 and by the FangSantos2019 directional delta method.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The singleton/finite/continuum regimes are just the cardinality cases of the active-set supremum law already implicit in ChernozhukovLeeRosen2013.

novelty_flags.label: Theorem 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The two-point tie bootstrap failure is a direct max-functional instance of FangSantos2019's bootstrap failure theorem, not a new published-axis kernel.

novelty_flags.label: Introduction / Related work
novelty_flags.one_line: The proposal says ChernozhukovLeeRosen2013 does not supply the active contact geometry, but that paper's V0 argmax/active set and sup-over-V0 Gaussian critical values are precisely that object.

novelty_flags.label: Theorem 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The proposal frames the witness as not computed by FangSantos2019, but the theorem is only the finite-dimensional max-map special case of their bootstrap failure criterion.

novelty_flags.label: §8 comparators
novelty_flags.one_line: The handoff promises strict tightening of ChernozhukovLeeRosen2013, FangSantos2019, AndrewsShi2013, and KaidoMolinariStoye2019, but no §8 statement is phrased as a strict tightening, iff frontier, or non-representability result against those papers.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 2
soundness_flags.one_line: The local-sequence drift clause uses sup over x notin C_L(P) without an empty-complement convention and does not specify the deterministic drift penalty it says enters the support functional.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 2
soundness_flags.one_line: After Conjecture 1, the three-phase law is a cardinality classification of sup over C_L(P), with no independent threshold or nonroutine mathematical step.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Replace the current kernel with a genuinely strict frontier against CLR, e.g. a computable observable rule showing when CLR/AIS critical values are conservative versus sharp for endpoint contact sets.
If keeping the bootstrap angle, state a corrected resampling or contact-set estimator with a theorem not implied by Fang-Santos, including tuning, validity regime, and a failure frontier.
Repair the local-drift clause by defining the outside-contact supremum convention and giving an explicit finite-gap local alternative formula.

recommended_next_step: Pivot: the current proposal is a relabelled intersection-bound/supremum-functional inference result already covered by CLR plus Fang-Santos.
